sections.paragraph:
Νινευή (inferior Νινευί [B-D-F §39, 1; s. Rob. 191f]), ἡ, indecl. (Gen 10:11f; Is 37:37; Jon 1:2 al.; Zeph 2:13 al. LXX; ViJon 2f [p. 83, 29 Sch.]; Hebr. נִינְוֵה) Nineveh, capital of the later Assyrian Empire Lk 11:32 v.l.; AcPlCor 2:29.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 674
